meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Faster Development for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Master AI-assisted debugging to eliminate slow development speed. A professional guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid shipping.
content.answer_block: Stop wasting hours chasing stack traces. By integrating Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for iterative terminal-based debugging, you can automate root-cause analysis. This workflow shifts your role from manual debugger to high-level architect, slashing development time by 60% while delivering production-ready code to your clients consistently.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and you’re staring at a cryptic error that makes no sense. You’ve been debugging for three hours, and your profit margin on this project is evaporating with every line of code you rewrite. The 'slow development' trap is the silent killer of every freelance career; it turns high-value engineering into a grind of frustration and missed deadlines.

But what if you could treat your codebase like a puzzle that solves itself? By leveraging advanced AI-driven debugging tools, you stop guessing and start executing. This isn't just about fixing bugs; it's about reclaiming your time and delivering professional, robust software at the speed of thought.

steps.title: Establish the Contextual Baseline
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the scope. Use Antigravity to map your current file dependencies to ensure AI has the full context of your project structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project directory, map all component dependencies, and identify potential circular references or tight coupling that could be causing runtime instability.

steps.title: Capture the Failure State
steps.description: Don't describe the bug; show it. Feed the raw stack trace and the state of your variables directly into your AI tooling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am experiencing a runtime error in [File Name]. Here is the stack trace: [Paste Trace]. Identify the specific line of code and the state mutation causing this failure.

steps.title: Isolate the Root Cause
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a focused search within your codebase to see if this error is an isolated incident or a symptom of a systemic architectural flaw.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a recursive search for all instances where the variable [Variable Name] is initialized. Does the current error pattern appear in other modules?

steps.title: Simulate the Fix
steps.description: Apply a sandboxed patch to verify the solution before committing to the main branch. This prevents 'fix-induced' regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a patch for [File Name] that resolves the error while adhering to clean code principles. Provide a brief explanation of why this solution is more performant.

steps.title: Automate Regression Testing
steps.description: Once fixed, generate a unit test that specifically targets the edge case that caused the bug, ensuring it never returns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest unit test file for [File Name] that covers the edge case identified in step 2. Ensure it validates the fix I just applied.

pro_tips: Always use the '--verbose' flag in Claude Code to understand the AI's reasoning process before applying changes.
Maintain a 'knowledge.md' file in your root; Antigravity can ingest this to remember client-specific preferences and tech stacks.
When debugging, ask the AI to 'explain the fix as a senior engineer,' which forces it to provide more robust, production-grade code.
Use 'claude commit --auto' only after you have manually verified the test suite passes, avoiding unnecessary noise in your git history.
Pipe your build logs directly into the AI context window to catch 'silent' failures that don't throw immediate stack traces.
content.next_steps: After completing this tutorial, refactor one of your legacy projects using this workflow to benchmark your speed increase, then integrate these prompts into your personal 'AI-Snippets' library.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs drain your billable hours. Master the art of AI-assisted development and start shipping your projects 3x faster than your competition. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Don't describe the bug; show it. Feed the raw stack trace and the state of your variables directly into your AI tooling.
Use Claude Code to perform a focused search within your codebase to see if this error is an isolated incident or a symptom of a systemic architectural flaw.
Apply a sandboxed patch to verify the solution before committing to the main branch. This prevents 'fix-induced' regressions.
Once fixed, generate a unit test that specifically targets the edge case that caused the bug, ensuring it never returns.
